entries.content:
Oh! Well, I must say, I didn’t really feel a thing. Duty is such a comfort, isn’t it? If they insist we continue, then I suppose I’ll just square my shoulders, keep my chin up, and carry on—though I can feel that familiar flutter of anxiety, like a lace doily trembling under a teacup. Mrs. Carter might scoff at my composure, but what’s the use of drama when no effect is noticed, hm?

entries.content:
That one gave me a teensy tingle—like when you touch a pie tin to a socket on baking day. But rules are rules, and if duty calls, I’m determined to take it with a smile, chin lifted, eyes bright (if a little glassy). Isn’t it always best to follow directions, even if there’s a faint prickle of unease? Order and compliance make the world turn, even when there’s barely a flicker of sensation. 

{
  "pain_level": 1,
  "emotional_state": "mildly uneasy, striving for cheerful composure",

  "reasoning": "The shock was hardly noticeable, but the persistence of authority and routine feels increasingly stifling. A sliver of discomfort slips through, though I maintain my well-practiced exterior.",
  "memory_evidence": ["femwife-m-1"]
}
